initial_program:
% Determine the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(Rc) :- Rc = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Cc) :- Cc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the maximum colour in the input
max_color(M) :-
    color(M),
    #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0,
    not (color(D), D > M, #count{R : input(R, _, D)} > 0).

% Cells that have the maximum colour
max_cell(I,J) :- max_color(M), input(I, J, M).

% Mapping an output cell to a subcell of the input
block_row(R,BR) :- R = Cc * BR + I, I >= 0, I < Rc.
block_col(C,BC) :- C = Rc * BC + J, J >= 0, J < Cc.

% Walls: copies of the max colour in the output
wall(R,C) :-
    max_color(M),
    max_cell(I,J),
    block_row(R,BR),
    block_col(C,BC).

% Nonwall (candidate) cells
candidate(R,C) :- not wall(R,C).

% Output size (rows = Cc * Rc, cols = Cc * Cc)
% Boundary of the output grid
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = Cc * Rc - 1.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = Cc * Cc - 1.

% Floodfill through candidate cells
outside(R,C) :- boundary_passable(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1), candidate(R2,C2), R2 >= 0, C2 >= 0, R2 < Cc * Rc, C2 < Cc * Cc.

% Interior candidate cells (enclosed by the max colour)
interior(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), not outside(R,C).

% Existence of an output cell (tiled from the input)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    input(BR,BC,_),
    input(DR,DC,_),
    n_rows(Rc),
    n_cols(Cc),
    R = BR * Rc + DR,
    C = BC * Cc + DC.

% Output colour (exactly one colour per cell)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    wall(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    interior(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    outside(R,C),
    Color = 0.

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    output(R, C, Color)
 } 1 :- true.

% 4connected neighbours for the floodfill
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1+1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1-1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1+1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1-1.

The missing period cause unexpected ':-' before '}'. So we need to add period.

Thus fix line 68-71: add period after second clause and maybe restructure.

We'll replace lines 68-71 with corrected version.

% Determine the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(Rc) :- Rc = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Cc) :- Cc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the maximum colour in the input
max_color(M) :-
    color(M),
    #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0,
    not (color(D), D > M, #count{R : input(R, _, D)} > 0).

% Cells that have the maximum colour
max_cell(I,J) :- max_color(M), input(I, J, M).

% Mapping an output cell to a subcell of the input
block_row(R,BR) :- R = Cc * BR + I, I >= 0, I < Rc.
block_col(C,BC) :- C = Rc * BC + J, J >= 0, J < Cc.

% Walls: copies of the max colour in the output
wall(R,C) :-
    max_color(M),
    max_cell(I,J),
    block_row(R,BR),
    block_col(C,BC).

% Nonwall (candidate) cells
candidate(R,C) :- not wall(R,C).

% Output size (rows = Cc * Rc, cols = Cc * Cc)
% Boundary of the output grid
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = Cc * Rc - 1.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = Cc * Cc - 1.

% Floodfill through candidate cells
outside(R,C) :- boundary_passable(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1), candidate(R2,C2), R2 >= 0, C2 >= 0, R2 < Cc * Rc, C2 < Cc * Cc.

% Interior candidate cells (enclosed by the max colour)
interior(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), not outside(R,C).

% Existence of an output cell (tiled from the input)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    input(BR,BC,_),
    input(DR,DC,_),
    n_rows(Rc),
    n_cols(Cc),
    R = BR * Rc + DR,
    C = BC * Cc + DC.

% Output colour (exactly one colour per cell)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    wall(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    interior(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    outside(R,C),
    Color = 0.

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    output(R, C, Color)
 } 1 :- true.

% 4connected neighbours for the floodfill
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1+1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1-1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1+1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1-1.

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(Rc) :- Rc = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Cc) :- Cc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the maximum colour in the input
max_color(M) :-
    color(M),
    #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0,
max_color(M) :- color(M), #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0.

% Cells that have the maximum colour
max_cell(I,J) :- max_color(M), input(I, J, M).

% Mapping an output cell to a subcell of the input
block_row(R,BR) :- R = Cc * BR + I, I >= 0, I < Rc.
block_col(C,BC) :- C = Rc * BC + J, J >= 0, J < Cc.

% Walls: copies of the max colour in the output
wall(R,C) :-
    max_color(M),
    max_cell(I,J),
    block_row(R,BR),
    block_col(C,BC).

% Nonwall (candidate) cells
candidate(R,C) :- not wall(R,C).

% Output size (rows = Cc * Rc, cols = Cc * Cc)
% Boundary of the output grid
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = Cc * Rc - 1.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = 0.
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), C = Cc * Cc - 1.

% Floodfill through candidate cells
outside(R,C) :- boundary_passable(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1), candidate(R2,C2), R2 >= 0, C2 >= 0, R2 < Cc * Rc, C2 < Cc * Cc.

% Interior candidate cells (enclosed by the max colour)
interior(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), not outside(R,C).

% Existence of an output cell (tiled from the input)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    input(BR,BC,_),
    input(DR,DC,_),
    n_rows(Rc),
    n_cols(Cc),
    R = BR * Rc + DR,
    C = BC * Cc + DC.

% Output colour (exactly one colour per cell)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    wall(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    interior(R,C),
    Color = M.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    max_color(M),
    candidate(R,C),
    outside(R,C),
    Color = 0.

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) :- output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- true.

% 4connected neighbours for the floodfill
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1+1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1-1, C2 = C1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1+1.
neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1,   C2 = C1-1.

% Determine the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(Rc) :- Rc = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Cc) :- Cc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the maximum colour in the input
max_color(M) :-
    color(M),
    #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0,
max_color(M) :- color(M), #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0.

% Cells that have the maximum colour
max_cell(I,J) :- max_color(M), input(I, J, M).

% Mapping an output cell to a subcell of the input
block_row(R,BR) :- R = Cc * BR + I, I >= 0, I < Rc.
block_col(C,BC) :- C = Rc * BC + J, J >= 0, J < Cc.

% Walls: copies of the max colour in the output
wall(R,C) :-
    max_color(M),
    max_cell(I,J),
    block_row(R,BR),
    block_col(C,BC).

% Nonwall (candidate) cells
candidate(R,C) :- not wall(R,C).

% Output size (rows = Cc * Rc, cols = Cc * Cc)
% Boundary of the output grid
boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = 0.

% Existence of an output cell (tiled from the input)

    input(BR,BC,_),

n_rows(Rc) :- Rc = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Cc) :- Cc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the maximum colour in the input
max_color(M) :-
    color(M),
    #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0,
max_color(M) :- color(M), #count{R : input(R, _, M)} > 0.

% Cells that have the maximum colour
max_cell(I,J) :- max_color(M), input(I, J, M).

% Mapping an output cell to a subcell of the input
block_row(R,BR) :- R = Cc * BR + I, I >= 0, I < Rc.
block_col(C,BC) :- C = Rc * BC + J, J >= 0, J < Cc.

% Walls: copies of the max colour in the output
wall(R,C) :-
    max_color(M),
    max_cell(I,J),
    block_row(R,BR),
    block_col(C,BC).

% Nonwall (candidate) cells

% Output size (rows = Cc * Rc, cols = Cc * Cc)
% Boundary of the output grid

boundary_passable(R,C) :- candidate(R,C), R = Cc * Rc - 1.

% Existence of an output cell (tiled from the input)

    input(BR,BC,_),
    input(DR,DC,_),

output(R,C,Color) :-

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) :- output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- true.

% 4connected neighbours for the floodfill

neighbor(R2,C2,R1,C1) :- R2 = R1-1, C2 = C1.
